sources.llm_summary: States that the Berkeley Master of Design is a professional graduate program in design for emerging technologies and that it is jointly offered by the College of Engineering and the College of Environmental Design.
Describes the Berkeley Master of Design as a three-semester professional graduate degree at UC Berkeley.
Identifies the site as Berkeley MDes / Master of Design at UC Berkeley.

derived_features.positioning_summary: A professional graduate program in design for emerging technologies that emphasizes interdisciplinary work, technical rigor, design theory, and social practice, with a studio-based format and explicit public-interest framing.

sources.llm_summary: States that Berkeley MDes is a professional graduate program in design for emerging technologies and that it emphasizes a critical approach to design and social practice to address pressing challenges that cut across disciplinary boundaries.
Describes the curriculum as connecting technical rigor, design theory, and social practice and preparing students for a broad range of creative and professional paths.
Describes the program as studio-based and professional, aimed at making emerging technologies matter, and notes its rootedness in Berkeley's history of thoughtful practice in the public interest.

derivation_notes.note: Used the explicit description of a professional graduate program in design for emerging technologies, plus the emphasis on disciplinary boundaries and social practice.
Used the explicit phrasing about technical rigor, design theory, and social practice to support the interdisciplinary and STEM-adjacent framing.
Used the explicit studio-based/professional wording and public-interest language to support professional and social-impact positioning.
Included interdisciplinary because the page explicitly says challenges cut across disciplinary boundaries.
Included professional because the page explicitly calls it a professional master's program and studio-based professional program.

Included stem_adjacent because the page explicitly mentions technical rigor and the program is for design; this directly supports adjacency to technical fields without overclaiming a pure engineering identity.
Included social_impact because the page explicitly references Berkeley's history of thoughtful practice in the public interest.
Did not add digital or ui_ux: the evidence mentions emerging technologies and design, but does not explicitly center digital products, platforms, interfaces, or UX.
Did not add research or physical_making: the evidence provided does not explicitly position the program around scholarly research output or fabrication/making.

sources.llm_summary: States that the MDes program runs over three semesters.
Describes the Berkeley Master of Design as a three-semester professional graduate degree.
States that over three semesters students complete the required core courses and electives.

derivation_notes.note: Used the explicit statement that the program is over three semesters.
Used the explicit description of the degree as a three-semester program to confirm the length.
Mapped the three-semester length to the allowed duration category three_semesters.

sources.llm_summary: States that MDes tuition is charged per unit, the per-unit fee is fixed for the cohort, the degree requires a minimum of 39 units, and the page provides an estimated total degree program cost table based on the minimum units.

Explains that the Degree Tuition & Fees section includes total tuition and campus fees for the minimum number of units over the three semesters, while the graduate student budget also includes living and other COA items.

sources.llm_title: Tuition Costs & Fees - Berkeley Graduate Division
sources.llm_summary: Describes graduate tuition and fees as estimates and notes that fees can include tuition, campus fee, transit fee, health insurance, nonresident fees, and other degree-related charges.

derivation_notes.note: Set to USD because the published program fee table is denominated in U.S. dollars.
Set to tuition_only because the source provides tuition and mandatory academic/program fees for the degree, not a full cost of attendance including living expenses.

Used the explicit estimated total degree program cost for the minimum 39 units: $2,109 per unit × 39 units = $82,251. This reflects tuition and required program/campus fees only; SHIP and Student Services Access Fee are excluded from the program fee table and not added here for comparability.

null — the page also presents a graduate student budget / cost of attendance that bundles living and other expenses, so it is not used for comparison_cost_usd; the tuition-and-fees subtotal is taken instead from the program fee table.
Set to 1 because the base currency is already USD, so no conversion is needed.
Used the current extraction date as the reference date for the no-op USD conversion.

derived_features.curriculum_summary: The program is a three-semester, studio-based curriculum requiring nine core courses plus three electives, with a summer outside-the-classroom experience expected between the first and second years. The published plan shows 39 minimum units and a mix of required core, capstone/thesis, and elective work.

core_courses.course_summary: Required core course that critically examines design mindset, skillset, and toolset through methods, strategy, and design history.
learning_outcomes: Examine the mindset, skillset, and toolset associated with design
Interweave design methods with strategy and history of design and technology
Approach design methods critically
Develop a framework for design practice and positioning

core_courses.course_summary: Required core course that helps students grasp contemporary design debates and become active participants in design discourse.
learning_outcomes: Grasp the nature of contemporary design debates
Become an active participant in design discourse
Critically engage with design positions and arguments
Develop a stronger critical stance on design issues

core_courses.course_summary: Required core course introducing technology design foundations.
Introduces MDes students to the key concepts of design studio and foundational principles and methods for studio practice.
learning_outcomes: Understand key concepts of design studio
Learn foundational principles and methods
Work collaboratively in a studio setting
Recognize the culture and life of a working studio

core_courses.course_summary: Required core course focused on designing emerging technologies, as listed in the Berkeley MDes program plan.
learning_outcomes: Design emerging technologies
Apply design thinking to new technologies
Develop concepts for technology-centered design work
Engage with the program's emerging technologies focus

core_courses.course_summary: Final studio course in the MDes program where students develop proposals for novel designed artifacts, services, environments, interfaces, or processes.
learning_outcomes: Develop proposals for novel designed artifacts
Explore physical objects, products, services, environments, curios, interfaces, or processes
Advance a final studio project
Produce work for the concluding MDes studio

elective_requirements.requirement_description: One elective appears in each of the three semesters in the published plan, for a total of three electives.
elective_requirements.course_summary: Flexible elective slot; the program notes that approved electives vary by scheduling availability and are updated regularly.

sources.llm_summary: States that the MDes program is studio-based and lists required courses including DES INV 200, 201, and 202.

States that over three semesters students complete nine required core courses and three electives, and shows the term-by-term plan including DES INV 210, 211, and 213 plus electives and a summer outside-the-classroom experience.
Explains that the list of approved electives changes based on scheduling availability and is updated regularly for MDes students each semester.

derivation_notes.note: Set true because the page explicitly states that students complete nine required core courses.
Set true because the page explicitly states that students complete three electives.

Set true because the electives page says the approved elective list changes based on scheduling availability and is updated regularly, indicating flexible elective choice within a published approval list.
Set true because the curriculum is described as studio-based and includes Studio Foundations and Thesis Studio in the term plan.
Set true because the term plan explicitly includes Capstone Portfolio and Thesis Studio.

Set true because the page states that in the summer between the first and second years students are expected to engage in an experience such as an internship, international design study, or community service outside the classroom.
Set to 39 based on the published minimum units required for the degree.
Set to semester_credit_hours because the curriculum uses units in a Berkeley semester-based program.

Set to partially_sequenced because the evidence provides a term-by-term plan for the required curriculum, but the overall program includes some elective flexibility and the named required items are not all presented as a fully rigid semester-by-semester sequence in the evidence available.
Summarized the three-semester structure, nine required core courses, three electives, and the summer outside-the-classroom experience from the program design page.

verification.verification_notes: Program data is supported by Berkeley MDes site excerpts and related UC Berkeley pages. The program appears to be a three-semester professional graduate degree with a tuition-only estimated total cost derived from the published minimum units and per-unit fee. Curriculum evidence supports required core courses, electives, studio sequence, thesis/capstone, and an outside-the-classroom summer experience.
